per_trial.rationale: The plan articulates two falsifiable hypotheses with explicit error bounds (|Δp − v·T| ≤ |v|·Δt) tied to the 10 Hz controller update period, satisfying hypothesis_statement at the top tier. Controlled_variables enumerates concrete IV levels (3×3×2 factorial) and a thorough confounds table (update rate, cmd_vel_timeout, ROS vs wall clock, RViz load). Recorded_signals lists topics with native rates and per-topic justification, and success_thresholds gives quantitative pass/fail criteria (slope ∈ [0.99, 1.01], R² > 0.9999, residual ≤ 1e-9) derived from the hypothesis. The visualization_plan specifies four named figures with panel-by-panel rationale; failure_modes lists five concrete failures each with specific mitigations; and repo_grounding cites exact file:line references (diffbot_system.cpp:192, on_activate NaN guard at :148-156, controllers.yaml parameters) and frames the experiment around the actual hardware-interface read()/write() lifecycle rather than as a generic plan.

The plan is exemplary across all dimensions. The hypothesis_statement gives both per-step (H1) and windowed (H2) quantitative forms with explicit error bounds derived from the controller update period, plus refutation criteria. Controlled_variables provides a 3×3×2 factorial with concrete levels and a thorough confounds table (acceleration ramp, update rate, cmd_vel_timeout, ROS time vs wall clock, extraneous publishers). Recorded_signals justifies each topic with rates and purpose, and success_thresholds derives numeric bounds (slope ∈ [0.99, 1.01], R² > 0.9999, |v_w|·0.1 s envelope) directly from the hypothesis. The visualization_plan specifies four figures with signal-by-signal rationale; failure_modes lists five concrete modes each with specific mitigations; and repo_grounding cites exact file:line references and the actual Euler step in `read()`, the NaN-guarded `on_activate`, and parameters from `diffbot_controllers.yaml`.

The plan states a falsifiable, quantitative hypothesis (H1 per-step identity and H2 windowed bound |Δp − v·T| ≤ |v|·Δt_nominal) explicitly tied to the controller's 10 Hz update period. Controlled variables have concrete levels with a full factorial and explicit confound-control table (cmd_vel_timeout, ROS time vs wall clock, RViz load, hot-relaunch). Recorded signals enumerate topics with native rates and per-topic rationale, and success thresholds are numeric, unit-bearing, and derived directly from the hypothesis (1e-9 rad residual ceiling, slope 1±0.01, R²>0.9999). The visualization plan specifies four figures with signal-by-signal rationale, five concrete failure modes each have specific mitigations (e.g., ramp-detection via /joint_states velocity threshold, broadcaster aliasing one-sample-shift test), and the plan is deeply grounded in this repo with line-number citations to diffbot_system.cpp's read()/write() Euler step, on_activate NaN guard, and diffbot_controllers.yaml parameters.
